In an informal style we  often 4 verbs     followed       by   infinitives I don't think so is more common than I think not, which  is rather formal.

I hope not. (     TdenT-hope so.) Hope is not used in this structure.

'Do you think it'll snow? 'I don't believe/suppose/think so.' Another possible short answer construction is I don't. . . so (►  585).

'Are we going to see Luke again? * I believe/suppose/hope not.' In short answers, most of these verbs can be followed by not (►  585).

3  short    answers:     I suppose         not.

1 hope it doesn't rain, (     I don't hope it rains.) This does not happen with hope.

Hello! I thought you weren't coming.

'Would you like a drink? 'I thought you'd never ask.' However, surprise is often expressed with I thought + negative.
